2604.07012.title: DTCRS: Dynamic Tree Construction for Recursive Summarization
2604.07012.paper_summary: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) mitigates the hallucination problem of Large Language Models (LLMs) by incorporating external knowledge. Recursive summarization constructs a hierarchical summary tree by clustering text chunks, integrating information from multiple parts of a document to provide evidence for abstractive questions involving multi-step reasoning. However, summary trees often contain a large number of redundant summary nodes, which not only increase construction time but may also negatively impact question answering. Moreover, recursive summarization is not suitable for all types of questions. We introduce DTCRS, a method that dynamically generates summary trees based on document structure and query semantics. DTCRS determines whether a summary tree is necessary by analyzing the question type. It then decomposes the question and uses the embeddings of sub-questions as initial cluster centers, reducing redundant summaries while improving the relevance between summaries and the question. Our approach significantly reduces summary tree construction time and achieves substantial improvements across three QA tasks. Additionally, we investigate the applicability of recursive summarization to different question types, providing valuable insights for future research.
2604.07012.paper_summary_zh: 檢索增強生成（RAG）通過整合外部知識來減輕大型語言模型（LLMs）的幻覺問題。遞歸摘要通過聚類文本片段構建層次摘要樹，整合文檔多個部分的信息，以提供涉及多步推理的抽象問題的證據。然而，摘要樹通常包含大量冗餘的摘要節點，這不僅增加了構建時間，還可能對問題回答產生負面影響。此外，遞歸摘要並不適用於所有類型的問題。我們介紹了DTCRS，一種基於文檔結構和查詢語義動態生成摘要樹的方法。DTCRS通過分析問題類型來確定摘要樹是否必要。然後，它將問題分解，並使用子問題的嵌入作為初始聚類中心，減少冗餘摘要，同時提高摘要與問題之間的相關性。我們的方法顯著減少了摘要樹的構建時間，並在三個QA任務中實現了實質性的改進。此外，我們還研究了遞歸摘要對不同問題類型的適用性，為未來的研究提供了有價值的見解。
2604.07012.author: Guanran Luo et.al.
2604.07012.authors: Guanran Luo, Zhongquan Jian, Wentao Qiu, Meihong Wang, Qingqiang Wu
